title: Ensure nftables is not in use with iptables

prose.en: Ensure nftables is not in use with iptables

description.en: `nftables` is a subsystem of the Linux kernel providing filtering and classification of network packets/datagrams/frames and is the successor to iptables.

rationale.en: Running both `iptables` and `nftables` may lead to conflict.

audit.en:
Run the following command to remove `nftables`:  

**\- OR \-**  

Run the following commands to stop and mask `nftables.service`:

```
# systemctl stop nftables.service
# systemctl mask nftables.service
```

```
# apt purge nftables
```

remediation.en:
Run the following command to remove `nftables`:  

**\- OR \-**  

Run the following commands to stop and mask `nftables.service`:

```
# systemctl stop nftables.service
# systemctl mask nftables.service
```

```
# apt purge nftables
```
